message: Thanks. I usually draw when I’m feeling overwhelmed. It helps me focus or... escape, I guess.
To be honest, I sometimes avoid things — schoolwork, talking to people, even fun stuff — because it just feels like too much all at once.
I think it’s part of my ADHD. When I know something will be hard or take a lot of energy, my brain kind of shuts down. So I just... don’t start at all.
I get that. Avoiding things doesn’t mean you’re lazy. It’s your brain trying to protect itself from burnout.
That means a lot to hear. I’ve felt like it was just me being unmotivated or making excuses. But talking to you makes it feel... okay, somehow.
Thanks for listening, %player%. I’m really glad I reached out. I think this might be the first time I’ve said that stuff out loud.
You’re not alone in this. And I’m always here to talk, sketch, or just hang out whenever you need it.
You’re awesome. Next time, maybe we can even draw something together?

actions.item: Help Jayden Build Confidence in Social Interactions
